# Questions in Livestream 1

Live Stream #1: The Debug Episode.

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=107" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47</a> | What are your thoughts on Josephus' *Against Apion*? Is it good evidence for the exodus? | Josephus is useful for events near his own time, but he is not treated as a reliable witness for much older history. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=202" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:22</a> | How did I come to know Christ? | He became a Christian at age seven while growing up in an evangelical Christian home. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=370" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">6:10</a> | Did you watch the Pharaohs' Golden Parade? If so, what was your reaction? | Yes. He thought it was a great spectacle and especially interesting as a modern ritual procession. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=446" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:26</a> | Have you looked into Ezekiel's prophecy concerning Nebuchadnezzar attacking Egypt? | Yes. He says Egypt was reduced to a rump state, though whether the prophecy is specifically about Nebuchadnezzar remains the debated question. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=606" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:06</a> | Who are your favorite philosophical influences? | He studied philosophy of religion and philosophy of science, was especially interested in Plato, and says teachers influenced him more than any one modern writer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=796" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:16</a> | What are the highs and lows of being interviewed? | Highs include good conversations; lows are moments when his memory fails and he later realizes he got something wrong. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=902" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:02</a> | Can you tell a fun or weird story from Egyptian history that people do not usually see in basic history? | He points to the humorous story of Wenamun, a priest or scribe sent to Tyre for lumber whose trip becomes a comedy of errors. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=995" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:35</a> | What is your favorite Egyptian creation myth? | He highlights the Memphite theology, where Ptah creates through thought and speech. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=1160" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:20</a> | What are your thoughts on the Levite hypothesis? | He is not persuaded by it and says there is not a good rationale for the hypothesis. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=1373" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:53</a> | If there is no evidence for King David's united monarchy, do you know a good scholar who talks about this? | He points to Eilat Mazar's work and says the question is now more about the monarchy's size and power than David's existence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=1526" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:26</a> | Is there any connection between the Book of the Dead and Genesis language about crawling on the belly or dust? | He does not see a connection; he says the Book of the Dead does not appear to play much role in biblical literature. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=1622" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:02</a> | Why did many Egyptian dynasties practice sibling marriage? | He says most supposed sibling marriages were not literal; true consanguineous royal marriages were rare and tied to succession or royal blood ideology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=1841" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:41</a> | Is Genesis 1-11 an ancient section of the Torah or a later Babylonian addition? | He rejects a later Babylonian addition and argues for origins in Late Bronze Age material, noting Egyptian vocabulary and lack of Old Iranian vocabulary. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=2056" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:16</a> | With a late date for the exodus, which pharaoh would you place at the time of Joseph? | He places Joseph in the Hyksos period but says the specific king cannot be identified with current evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=2232" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:12</a> | What are your thoughts on Tell el-Hammam being Sodom? | He says it is possible but the case is not strong enough yet and needs more work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=2491" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:31</a> | What books would you recommend for an apologist making a case for the exodus? | He recommends recent background studies, *Did I Not Bring My People Out of Egypt?*, Kitchen's *On the Reliability of the Old Testament*, and his own work on the Ark. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=2739" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:39</a> | Will scholarship on the exodus change over the next few decades as it has for the Davidic monarchy? | Yes, but he stresses careful handling of evidence and avoiding overstated claims. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=2913" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:33</a> | When was Avaris abandoned, and how can we know the people who became Israelites lived there and left? | Avaris was abandoned in early to mid Dynasty 19; the Israelite connection is circumstantial because they would not yet have been called Israelites. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=3139" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:19</a> | Did Hebrew exist as a distinct language at the time of the exodus, or was it an early proto-Canaanite dialect? | He says Hebrew likely existed as a distinct Western Semitic dialect already being spoken at Avaris. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=3308" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:08</a> | When do you think the battle of Genesis 14 occurred if Amraphel of Shinar is Hammurabi? | The date remains uncertain; he places it sometime after Hammurabi's alliance with the Elamites and before Hammurabi later attacked them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=3506" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:26</a> | What do you think happened to Egypt after the exodus? | He argues Egypt was not defenseless, but lost much of its chariot force, made peace with the Hittites, and began losing its grip on Canaan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=3818" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:38</a> | Is the "wings of the dawn" in Psalm 139:9 related to winged sun disk imagery like Hezekiah's seal? | Very possibly. He says Israelite texts and seals could adapt ancient Near Eastern divine symbols for Israelite purposes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=4118" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:38</a> | If Yahu of Shasu is Yahweh, would that cause problems? | No. He cautions that Yahu may be a place or deity name, and says it would not create a theological problem either way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/uduERXxcb1E?t=4440" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:00</a> | Is there evidence of Israelites practicing monotheism or something similar in the Levant? | The quick answer is no; he says monotheism begins at Sinai but Israel took time to live consistently with it. |
